name: library_patron_privacy_wifi_probe
premise: Library director Amina Yusuf must decide whether to enable Wi-Fi probe logging after a violent threat was emailed from a device seen near the children’s wing. Digital rights volunteer Rafael Soto warns probe logging will capture identifiers of unhoused patrons who rely on the library and may be disproportionately surveilled. Police say logs could narrow suspects, but the vendor cannot guarantee the data won’t be retained beyond thirty days. If Amina refuses, staff fear another incident and the mayor may cut funding. If she enables logging, the library’s promise of safe access for all may be broken for years.

agents.name: Amina Yusuf
agents.identity: I am the public library director responsible for patron safety, privacy commitments, and ensuring access is not chilled by surveillance measures.
agents.goal: I want to protect staff and children without turning the library into a de facto tracking zone for vulnerable patrons.

agents.name: Rafael Soto
agents.identity: I am a digital rights volunteer advising community groups on privacy harms when public institutions collect device identifiers under pressure.
agents.goal: I want probe logging rejected and alternative safety steps used, because surveillance burdens fall unevenly and are rarely rolled back once deployed.

choices: I will enable probe logging with strict retention limits and public notice, accepting some privacy loss to aid threat investigation and deter harm.
I will refuse probe logging and instead add visible security staffing and anonymous tip channels, accepting slower attribution and political backlash.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The mayor wants an immediate safety plan and police are requesting logs. Amina Yusuf must choose between enabling Wi-Fi probe logging or refusing and using non-tracking safety measures. What does Amina Yusuf do?
